_purpose: Append-only cache of citation verification results. Each line is one JSON object recording a single fetch-and-compare event. Used by the adversarial-reviewer agent to avoid repeatedly re-fetching arXiv / DOI metadata for unchanged bibitems. One line per (bibkey, bibitem_content_hash) pair; most recent wins.

_entry_format.paper: string — paper_key where this bibitem appears (if source is a paper rather than the global registry)
_entry_format.bibitem_hash: string — sha256 of the normalized bibitem text (authors + title + arxiv_id + venue, lowercase, whitespace-collapsed) so we can detect when a bibitem was edited
_entry_format.arxiv_id: string|null — arxiv ID as written in the bibitem
_entry_format.fetched_title: string|null — title returned by arxiv / doi when fetched
_entry_format.fetched_authors: string|null — first-author et al. as returned
_entry_format.verdict: match | wrong_target | wrong_author | wrong_title | wrong_venue | fetch_failed
_entry_format.reviewer: string — who or what produced this record (e.g. adversarial-reviewer / claims-reviewer / human:jroehm)

_staleness_policy: Entries older than 90 days should be re-verified even if bibitem_hash matches (catches cases where a paper is updated on arXiv without title change, or a DOI now resolves to a different record). The adversarial-reviewer is responsible for applying this policy.
